title: Apple Online & Apple Education Store: 8% Cashback (Uncapped, Exclusions Apply, See Terms) @ TopCashback AU
body:
Deal: Apple Online & Apple Education Store: 8% Cashback (Uncapped, Exclusions Apply, See Terms) @ TopCashback AU

[rpb] Such a shame the prices went up this morning. I was hoping to use this deal tomorrow but unlikely now :(
[life is suffering] ya 8% won't cut it prices went 10-20% up
[rkm] If looking for a macbook, better prices are in Officeworks/JB/Harvey/other authorised stores. Surprised to see the % increase on these products on apple store overnight.
[shxhshzhz] 8% cashback on prices increased by much more than 8% 🙁 . Better prices at other stores. E.g.
iPad A16 $479 @ The Good Guys
$749 @ Apple store
[taki] $749 is ridiculous for less RAM & no AI :(
[Roe Jogan] still cheaper from jbhifi, get em while u can lol
1597 for air 13 m5
and 2199 for pro 14 m5
[taki] Yes, apple -8% is too little back from their gross margin of 47.86% for overpriced & no AI ;)
[FireRunner] 8% back right after they jacked up the MacBooks
[onyx981] Aside from global ram/ssd price increases, assuming the price hikes were a response to their 10% drop in stock prices this month which seem to be recovering after the hikes. Their ram and ssd prices were already insanely marked up and they could have absorbed some of the costs rather than a flat 20% increase.
[netsurfer] Apple share price dropped 6% after yesterday's announcement on the price hike. For now, Apple is only willing to absorb the cost for iPhones, which is Apple's bread and butter.
Normally, Apple have a very high bargaining power, but at the moment, major RAM and flash memory providers don't care.
[FujinShu] Yea, more AI companies are waving their literal sausages all over the bargaining table now, and the flash memory makers can’t get enough of them lol.
